resource.name: sessions/18400269701079164058
resource.title: Task 11: File Readers
resource.prompt:
IMPORTANT: You have access to the context7 MCP server. Before writing ANY Rust code, use context7 to look up the latest documentation for the crates you are using:
- Use "Resolve Context7 Library ID" to find library IDs, then "Query Documentation" to get up-to-date API docs and examples.
- Look up: "windows-rs" (for Win32 API bindings), "tokio" (for async runtime), "thiserror" (for error types), "bitflags" (for flag enums), "byteorder" (for binary parsing).
- For Phase 2 tasks, also look up: "egui" and "eframe" (for GUI), "serde" (for serialization), "configparser" (for INI parsing).
- Always verify crate APIs against context7 docs rather than guessing from memory.

Port WizWalker file readers from Python to Rust.

Source files:
- https://github.com/LaurenzLikeThat/wizwalker/blob/development/wizwalker/file_readers/wad.py → wizwalker/src/file_readers/wad.rs (WAD archive parser)
- https://github.com/LaurenzLikeThat/wizwalker/blob/development/wizwalker/file_readers/nif.py → wizwalker/src/file_readers/nif.rs (NIF 3D file parser)
- https://github.com/LaurenzLikeThat/wizwalker/blob/development/wizwalker/file_readers/cache_handler.py → wizwalker/src/file_readers/cache_handler.rs

Pure data parsers, no Win32 dependency. Use std::io + byteorder crate. Update file_readers/mod.rs.
